3 - Freedom Funders Launch Plan.mp4

Thank you. All right. So this is a funnel that, uh, it's really, uh, really a great case study. So we built this whole funnel out for Colin, um, freedom funders and essentially. Colin was running a high-ticket funnel. um, call funnel straight to his 8k backend. And it's something that has done pretty good overall. And I want to walk you guys through the whole launch plan. As always, we have our agenda, products and offers, competitors, funnel review, Facebook projections. Um, so. You have the collaboration between us, Collin and Client Accelerators, to launch a low-ticket funnel for Collin's credit repair offer. Client Accelerators is going to handle the entire process, including building the funnel, setting up automation, managing ad campaigns. You know, targeting the ideal customer profile.

The primary goal is to liquidate maximum ad spend on day one while funneling leads into the backend offer after their credit has been repaired. So you have product and offers. So the front end is a $39 credit mastery book. We actually ended up finding that the $29 offer ended up working a little bit better. Then we had a bump offer— or we have a bump offer at $47 a month. This one has absolutely crushed it. We're at about a 51% take rate, very high. Very high retention rate as well. So this is something I teach in the course. If you are running a bump offer, ideally, you want to make it where it's recurring, if possible, if you have the bandwidth, if you have the product, make it recurring, you're going to get a much higher revenue per order versus just having, you know, a one-time offer.

There, it's going to definitely help you there. OT01 was 197 for six months. Downsell one is $97 for 12 months. OTO two is your business credit mastery at $97, and OTO three is your business funding plan. Okay? So our ideal clients and buyer persona is Emily. She's a 29-year-old recent college graduate, dreams of owning her first home. She works as a marketing assistant, but struggles with low credit score due to student loans. She's problem aware she wants to increase her credit score to 730 plus within 90 days to qualify for a mortgage. Buy your dream home. You know, some of her fears, she fears the process will take too long or won't be effective. You know, the credit mastery book simplifies concepts and gives clear, actionable steps to reach a 730 plus score in 90 days.

Is a 42-year-old small business owner who wants to expand his business. His credit score was impacted during the pandemic and he now struggles to secure loans. Marcus has about investing time and money into credit repair because he fears it won't yield the desired results. He's solution-aware. Sarah is a 34-year-old teacher. She doubts whether she can build a business credit. She feels she lacks the knowledge and support. Then you got James. You know, James is a 50-year-old corporate manager who wants to retire early by opening a consulting business. He has been denied credit funding due to a lack of business credit history. Um, he obtains $50,000 plus some business funding to transition into consulting, leave his corporate job. He's scared of losing his savings and not achieving his early retirement plans.

And you have Laura. So let's dive into the competitors. So you have Credit Secrets, which is a huge offer. It's done a couple hundred million dollars from a buddy of mine. You have Credit Repair Cloud. Derek A. Harper, Credit Suite, Will Roundtree, Jose Rodriguez. So you have the funnel review, page view, OT01, Downsell 1, OT02, OT03. And you have the thank you page. Essentially, what it looks like. Now your projections. So we projected about a $5 cost per click. Um, front-end conversion rate— this is actually incorrect. We projected a—uh— this is based upon the add-to-cart rate. So overall, our front-end conversion rate was about 2%. That we projected here. The bump order conversion rate was about 35%. We beat those projections. You have month one, month two, month three.

So we had about 10% on the OT01. 15% on OTO, the downsell. 12% on OTO2, 12% on OTO3. About $167 cost per unique customer. AOV is about 106. Liquidation rate was about 64%. 37 book calls. Set calls is about 26. Calls per call about 270. So roll back in revenue. This was a very big back-end revenue play. We want people to ascend into his back-end program. Okay. Then we had YouTube. So you have a $5,000 spend, cost per click of about $4, CPMs of $61, a front-end conversion rate of 13%, bump order, same thing. So the big piece was you had a ton of. uh recurring here, so we needed to track what day one, day 60, and day 90 look like.

So what did that actually look like over, you know, the different periods of time? Where did the AOV actually go to from here? So you can see you went from 109 to 157 to 181. And then we did quarterly projections from here. And the creative strategy, you know, so it doesn't have to be too complicated. Financial freedom starts with your credit. I wish you had 750 plus. A whole bunch of different creatives. Then we did a whole bunch of images here as well. You know, stop letting bad credit hold you back.

So here's the actual Facebook campaign rollout. We did 25 to 60 US. Creative testing. You know, we were trying to find creative winners from here. Uh, audience testing.

So these were all of the different audiences that we essentially put together. Here is the rollout strategy. So max conversions, male, female, 25 to 54. We did in-market business loans, investment services, custom intent audience, business funding, credit score improvement seekers. Retargeting viewed video as ad. Same thing. We had branded search as well. And you have your risks and your proposed solutions. So, you know, if the front end hits one, one and a half percent or lower, which gives us a CPA of $334 and an AOV of 130. $130. $3. 70, which only allows us to liquidate. You know, we wanted to test some different leads, test a couple of different price points. If the AOV was not above $106, we wanted to test different price points, test different products for the upsells.

The monetization window to 60 days. And then the third biggest risk was low buyer lead to book call ratio, the speed to lead, and using an AI setter to text all the buyers who didn't book a call. So when we first launched this, CPCs were high around $8, front-end ROAS was stuck at 0. 58x, and book calls cost over $500 each. To make matters worse, back-end sales were non-existent in the first month. The main hurdle was proving that a low-ticket entry offer could not only cover ad spend, but could also feed a profitable backend. So, we rolled out fresh creative angles and tighter targeting. We brought CPCs down from $8 to under $6. Boosting the order value. The checkout flow and upsells were reworked.

Lifting AOV from 152 to almost. And we got this to about $200. So that needs to be updated. Nurturing buyers. We structured a follow-up process to help convert more buyers into book calls. 22% to nearly 40%. And then the back end sales push with more qualified calls coming in. The back end team. could finally close deals, creating meaningful revenue. Beyond the front end. So we went from 63 in one month to about 126 in month three. Total revenue rose from nine and a half to over 58,000 in cash collected. Profit turned around quickly. We went from a 7K loss to 38K in profit by month three. ROAS went from 0. 58 to about 3. 66 on total contract value.

So we had a back-end break for you. The funnel evolved from a front-end loss into a back-end revenue driver. You know, this allowed us to really... add another funnel into Colin's repertoire to bring new people in, and we really built up a nice monthly recurring revenue stream for Colin as well. So, a highly successful launch did very very well for us.
